task: Write a Python function longest_word(s) returning the longest whitespace-separated word in the string. On ties return the last such word, not the first. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all requirements, but only one edge case is mentioned.

results.got: raised ValueError('max() iterable argument is empty')

trace: To solve the task, we first split the input string into words. We then find the maximum length of any word in the list using a generator expression with the built-in `max()` function. Finally, we use another generator expression (with lambda function) to filter out shorter words when finding the longest one, following the stated direction of returning the last occurrence or longest run. This ensures that ties are broken in favor of the last such word as specified in the criteria.
